topic: help writing a happy birthday text for his mom

turns.content: ok so this is kinda embarrassing but its my moms birthday tomorrow and I wanna send her a really nice text but im terrible at writin stuff like that can you help me out

Not embarrassing at all — wanting to send your mom something nice is a good thing. I'd be happy to help. Tell me a little about your mom and what you'd want to say. Like, is she more of a sentimental type or does she appreciate humor? And do you want it to be short and sweet or more of a longer message?

shes definitely sentimental bro like she cries at hallmark commercials lol but I dont want it to be too long cuz its a text not a novel you know. I just want her to know shes the best and that I appreciate everything shes done for me growin up

Got it — short, heartfelt, and genuine. Here's a draft for you:

"Happy birthday Mom! You're the hardest working person I know and I wouldn't be where I am without you. Thanks for always having my back and believing in me. I love you and I hope today is all about you for once. You deserve it."

How's that feel? I can adjust the tone or add anything specific if you want.
dude thats actually really good. I have dyslexia so writin stuff like that takes me forever and it never comes out right when I try to do it myself but thats pretty much exactly what I wanted to say

Hey, you knew exactly what you wanted to say — you just needed help getting it down in words, and there's nothing wrong with that. The feelings behind it are all yours, I just organized them a little. Do you want to add anything personal, like a specific memory or something you two do together? That kind of thing can really make it hit different for a mom.

oh thats a good idea yeah so when I was a kid she used to take me and my sister to get ice cream at this spot in dorchester after church every sunday and its one of my favorite memories I could say somethin about that

That's perfect. Here's an updated version:

"Happy birthday Mom! You're the hardest working person I know and I wouldn't be where I am without you. I still think about those Sunday ice cream trips after church — those are some of my best memories growing up. Thanks for always having my back and believing in me. I love you and I hope today is all about you for once. You deserve it."

That personal touch makes it way more meaningful. Want to change anything?
bro thats gonna make her cry guaranteed lol but in a good way. can you make the end a little more casual though like more how I would actually talk cuz she knows I dont write like that

Good call, you want it to sound like you. Here's the updated ending:

"Happy birthday Mom! You're the hardest working person I know and I wouldn't be where I am without you. I still think about those Sunday ice cream trips after church — those are some of my best memories growing up. Thanks for always having my back. Love you more than you know. Have the best day you deserve it!!"

A little more relaxed at the end but still hits the same. How's that?
thats perfect bro im copyin that right now. shes gonna love it. I might also get her flowers in the mornin before work you think thats too much

Too much? No way, that's the perfect combo. The text will make her emotional and then flowers on top of that? She's going to be so happy. You don't even need to go crazy with it — just a nice simple bouquet from a local shop or even a grocery store will do the job. It's the thought that counts, and the fact that you're putting this much effort in says a lot. She's lucky to have you.

yeah I was thinkin just somethin simple from the stop and shop by my place they always got those bouquets by the register for like 15 bucks. do I gotta write somethin on the card too or can I just leave it blank since shes already gettin the text

I'd write something short on the card, even just "Happy birthday Mom, love Jake" — a blank card looks a little unfinished. It doesn't have to be a whole message since you've got the text covering that. Just your name and a quick line is enough. She'll probably keep the card on the fridge or something, moms love that stuff.
appreciate it man for real. I always stress about this stuff cuz the writin part is hard for me and I dont want it to come out wrong but you made it easy. thanks bro

Anytime, seriously. You clearly care a lot about your mom and that comes through no matter how the words are arranged. Happy to help whenever you need to write something — just hit me up. Hope your mom has an awesome birthday tomorrow!
